channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Password Security Explained: Why Length Beats Complexity (and How Entropy Works)
item.description: Most password advice is wrong — or at least, not based on what actually makes passwords hard to crack. Rules like "use a capital letter, a number, and a symbol" produce passwords like P@ssword1,...

item.title: Character Limits That Matter: Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Meta Descriptions, and More
item.description: Character limits are everywhere, and they're rarely intuitive. Twitter is 280, but tweets with images feel different. LinkedIn gives you 3,000 characters for posts, but only 220 display before...

item.title: arrayJoin in ClickHouse: Why Your Rows Are Duplicating (and How to Control It)
item.description: When working with arrays in ClickHouse, arrayJoin feels straightforward. Until your query suddenly returns far more rows than expected. The Use Case Let’s say you have a table like this: CREATE...

item.title: How to Trim and Cut Video with FFmpeg (CLI + API Guide)
item.description: Originally published at ffmpeg-micro.com. You need to extract a 30-second clip from a 2-hour video. Or trim the first 5 seconds of dead air. Or cut everything after the outro. FFmpeg handles all...

item.title: Python Type Hints That Actually Catch Bugs (Not Just Satisfy mypy)
item.description: Most type hint guides teach you syntax. This one is about which annotations actually prevent bugs. After two years of adding types to codebases that didn't have them — and watching which...

item.title: SQL Subqueries vs CTEs: What I Wish Someone Told Me When I Was Learning SQL
item.description: When you first start learning SQL, everything feels simple. You write basic SELECT queries, maybe add a WHERE, and life is good. Then suddenly you meet things like subqueries and CTEs, and it...

item.title: AI didn't save $500k. A test suite did.
item.description: Behind every successful AI rewrite story, there is an unsung hero. And it's never the AI. Chances are you've already come across the Reco story. Their AI tool rewrote JSONata from JavaScript to Go...

item.title: 🔐 From Passwords to Private Keys: Understanding Solana Identity the Web2 Way
item.description: 📦 Account = Your Database Record (Owned by You) In Web2: Your data lives in a database row (e.g., user profile, balance) The company owns and controls that database In Solana: An account is a...

item.title: Map and Set in JavaScript
item.description: Javascript comes from a long days where we manage everything with just objects and arrays, in ES6 a map and set data structures are introduced which provide more specialized ways to handle data...

item.title: CLAUDE.md for Node.js: 6 Rules That Stop AI From Generating Bad Backend Code
item.description: CLAUDE.md for Node.js: 6 Rules That Stop AI From Generating Bad Backend Code You ask Claude to "add a POST /users endpoint" and you get back: A .then().catch() chain mixed with await in the same...

item.title: It’s a busy time for sci-fi, but don’t miss Aphelion
item.description: The last few weeks have bordered on overwhelming for science fiction fans. While Project Hail Mary is dominating the box office, For All Mankind is currently in the midst of its penultimate...

item.title: The Bloomberg Terminal Is Getting an AI Makeover, Like It or Not
item.description: WIRED spoke with Bloomberg's chief technology officer about the big, chatbot-style changes coming to the iconic platform for traders.

item.title: Chinese Silk Typhoon Hacker Extradited to U.S. Over COVID Research Cyberattacks
item.description: A Chinese national accused of being a member of the Silk Typhoon hacking group has been extradited to the U.S. from Italy. Xu Zewei, 34, was arrested in July 2025 by Italian authorities for his...

item.title: Microsoft Office can now be controlled with Logitech’s MX Creative Console
item.description: Logitech has announced a new suite of Productivity Plugins for its entire MX line of accessories, including its Stream Deck alternative, the MX Creative Console. Since the console launched in...

item.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
item.description: An administrative role meant for artificial intelligence (AI) agents within Microsoft Entra ID could enable privilege escalation and identity takeover attacks, according to new findings from...

item.title: Microsoft Confirms Active Exploitation of Windows Shell CVE-2026-32202
item.description: Microsoft on Monday revised its advisory for a now-patched, high-severity security flaw impacting Windows Shell to acknowledge that it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in...

item.title: Jury selection in Musk v. Altman: ‘People don’t like him’
item.description: On Monday, the courtroom battle between Elon Musk and Sam Altman over alleged broken promises at OpenAI started, as usual, with jury selection. The only tricky part? A lot of the prospective...

item.title: Some Musk v. Altman Jurors Don't Like Elon Musk
item.description: Musk’s lawsuit challenges OpenAI’s evolution under Sam Altman. But during jury selection, several potential jurors voiced negative views of Musk himself.

item.title: Adaptive Ultrasound Imaging with Physics-Informed NV-Raw2Insights-US AI
Google is testing AI chatbot search for YouTube
item.description: Google is trying out an AI Mode-like search experience for YouTube. The company is now testing "a new way to search on YouTube that feels more like a conversation," with results pulling in things...

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
item.description: After a yearslong legal feud, Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are heading to trial this week in Northern California in a case that could have sweeping consequences. Ahead of OpenAI’s highly...

item.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
item.description: If you're one of millions using element-data, it's time to check for compromise.

item.title: Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux
item.description: One of the most popular Linux distributions is about to get an influx of AI features. As reported by Phoronix, Jon Seager, VP of engineering at Ubuntu developer Canonical, shared a blog post on...

item.title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins
item.description: The move comes as the trial for Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI kicks off in federal court in Oakland.

item.title: Trump demands ABC fire Jimmy Kimmel
item.description: President Donald Trump is calling for Disney to fire Jimmy Kimmel. On Thursday, Kimmel joked that Melania Trump had looked like an "expectant widow" in a skit about the upcoming White House...

item.title: Google employees ask Sundar Pichai to say no to classified military AI use
item.description: Over 600 Google employees signed a letter to CEO Sundar Pichai demanding that Google block the Pentagon from using its AI models for classified purposes, reports the The Washington Post. Its...

item.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
item.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

item.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
item.description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Conspiracy theories are swirling about the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting
item.description: On Saturday evening, a room full of journalists, media personalities, and senior members of Donald Trump's administration descended into chaos as gunshots rang out at the White House...

item.title: Why the Steam Controller is (and isn’t) a big deal
item.description: Most PC gamers already have a controller they love using with Steam - a Sony DualSense, a 8BitDo Ultimate, a Nintendo Switch Pro, or something else. Part of that love comes from Steam treating...
